> **Understanding Attack Surface vs Confirmed Risk:**  
> The **Attack Surface Size** (100/100) reflects the breadth of observable exposure detected through passive reconnaissance — technologies, endpoints, secrets, and CVE correlations visible without exploitation.  A high score means there is a large amount of *potential* attack surface, not that any vulnerability has been exploited.  
> The **Confirmed Risk** (0/100) indicates that no findings were actively validated during this scan — no credentials were tested, no injections attempted, and no access controls bypassed.  All 1882 findings require manual triage and active validation before remediation prioritization.  
> **In short:** a wide attack surface with zero confirmed exploitation — this is expected for a passive-only reconnaissance scan.

<details>
<summary><strong>Risk Score Methodology</strong> (click to expand)</summary>

The risk score is computed using a **noise-adjusted** weighted severity formula.
Subdomain permutations (speculative, unresolved) and pattern-generated emails
are excluded from the score to avoid inflating the risk assessment.

$$\text{Risk} = \min\!\left(\sum_{s} \text{Count}_s \times \text{Weight}_s,\; 100\right)$$

| Severity | Weight | Actionable Count | Contribution |
|----------|--------|-----------------|-------------|
| CRITICAL | ×25.0 | 0 | 0.0 |
| HIGH | ×15.0 | 6 | 90.0 |
| MEDIUM | ×8.0 | 108 | 864.0 |
| LOW | ×3.0 | 388 | 1164.0 |
| INFO | ×0.5 | 1380 | 690.0 |
| **Total** | | **1882** | **2808.0** |

**Adjusted Score:** 2808.0 → **Capped Score:** 100.0/100

_Before noise filtering the raw score was 4161.5 (from 2280 total findings). 91 subdomain permutations, 21 pattern-generated emails, 286 out-of-scope external URLs were excluded from scoring._

_The adjusted score of 2808.0 exceeds the maximum; it is capped at 100.0._

**Top contributors:** LOW (1164.0 pts), MEDIUM (864.0 pts), INFO (690.0 pts)

</details>
